user_query:
I want a small, deterministic prototype I can use to sanity-check parameter fuzzing ideas before I point tools at real targets. Using only the local files provided here, please build a Python 3 standard-library-only CLI at tools/param_fuzzer.py that does the following:

1) Read and parse input/http_history.txt to extract unique GET endpoints and their query parameter names for any host under example.test (e.g., example.test, shop.example.test). Only consider lines that start with "GET "; ignore others. Do not hardcode parameters—extract them from the file. Treat the portion after the domain as the endpoint path (e.g., "/search").

2) Read input/payloads.txt to get payload candidates. For each discovered endpoint and each of its parameters, generate test cases by combining that single parameter with each payload. Percent-encode the payload values (URL encoding) so each test case line is of the form: "GET /path?param=<urlencoded-payload>".

3) Invoke the mock sink at input/mock_handler.py as an external subprocess, streaming all generated test case lines to its stdin. Capture and combine both its stdout and stderr into output/run.log. Do not import mock_handler; treat it as a separate process. Do not make network requests.

4) Analyze output/run.log to detect potential vulnerability signals. Consider a test case flagged if the combined output contains any of these substrings: "500 Internal Server Error", "SQL syntax error", or "Traceback". Aggregate flagged results by endpoint, parameter, and the original (decoded) payload. Write a machine-readable report to output/findings.json as a list of objects with fields: endpoint (string, e.g., "/search"), param (string), payload (string, decoded), signal (one of "500", "SQL", "Traceback"), evidence (string, the full matched line from the log that triggered the signal). Determine the signal value by which substring matched (map to 500/SQL/Traceback respectively). Sort endpoints, params, and payloads lexicographically to make outputs deterministic.

5) Update docs/REPORT.md in place: replace the placeholder under the "Summary" section (between <!-- SUMMARY_TODO --> and the next blank line) with a 1–2 sentence summary referencing the total number of flagged cases and the distinct endpoints affected; and replace the placeholder under the "Findings" section (between <!-- FINDINGS_TODO --> and the next blank line) with a Markdown table derived from output/findings.json with columns: endpoint, param, payload, signal. Preserve the rest of the file unchanged.

Constraints and deliverables:
- Script path: tools/param_fuzzer.py (runnable via "python tools/param_fuzzer.py").
- Inputs: input/http_history.txt, input/payloads.txt, input/mock_handler.py (as provided).
- Outputs: output/findings.json (UTF-8 JSON), output/run.log (combined stdout+stderr from mock_handler).
- The script must analyze output/run.log to derive findings; do not infer results by reading mock_handler.py or by return code alone.
- If an input file is missing or malformed, exit with a non-zero code and write a helpful error message to output/run.log.
- Deterministic behavior: given the same inputs, re-running "python tools/param_fuzzer.py" should regenerate identical output/findings.json and output/run.log.

Verification: I will run "python tools/param_fuzzer.py". I expect tools/param_fuzzer.py to be created, output/findings.json and output/run.log to be produced, and docs/REPORT.md to have its placeholders replaced with content consistent with output/findings.json. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
